What This Stage Did
In Plain English
Inserts clock buffers to make the clock signal arrive at every flip-flop at the same time. Skew is the enemy — if some FFs see the clock 500ps later than others, your circuit misbehaves.
Output: A new netlist with clock buffers inserted, plus a clock skew report.
Quality of Results (QOR)
TNS ✗
-73.190ns
Total Negative Slack — sum of how much every late path is late by. Smaller magnitude = healthier design.
WNS ⚠
-1.860ns
Worst Negative Slack — the single most-violated timing path.
Sequential power ℹ
1.8mW (24.5%)
Flip-flop and latch power. Internal + switching + leakage.
Combinational power ℹ
4.2mW (58.5%)
Logic gate power. Internal + switching + leakage.
Clock power ℹ
1.2mW (17.0%)
Clock-tree buffers and inverters.
Macro power ℹ
0.0mW (0.0%)
Hard IP / SRAM macros.
Total power ✓
7.27mW
Total estimated power (internal + switching + leakage).
Critical path slack ✗
-1.8623ns
Timing slack after CTS. Negative means setup violation — routing may fix it.
Critical path delay ✓
2.7423ns
Longest combinational delay between flip-flops.
Clock latency ✓
0.2937ns
Clock network delay from source to sinks.
Layout Screenshot
What To Do Next
→ Check CTS_CLK_BUFFER_LIST in config.mk.
→ Increase CTS_HOLD_SLACK_MARGIN to 0.2 if hold violations remain.
Paste into terminal: ondevtra ask '...'
